tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto
tasks.hook_line: You're IN Kyoto right now — 3 days left to buy 4 pairs of glasses.
tasks.reward: Locks in where to go tomorrow so you don't waste daylight wandering
nuggets: Zoff and JINS are major chains, ~¥5000-10000/pair
Kyoto Station has 3-4 eyewear stores in mall
tasks.system_prompt: I'm currently in Kyoto, Japan (day 12 of 16-day trip, leaving April 7). I want to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses while here. Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto — preferably chains like Zoff or JINS that do same-day service and have English-speaking staff. I need: store names, Google Maps links, approximate price ranges, and whether they accept US prescriptions. I have US insurance that may cover some cost.

tasks.first_domino: Check insurance app for vision coverage details
tasks.hook_line: Can you get 4 pairs for the price of 1 US pair if insurance reimburses?
tasks.reward: Confirms whether this saves $400+ or just creates paperwork hassle
nuggets: US vision plans often reimburse foreign purchases
Prescription format may differ US vs Japan
tasks.system_prompt: I'm buying 4 pairs of eyeglasses in Japan (currently in Kyoto, leaving April 7). I have US vision insurance. Help me figure out: (1) Do I need a new prescription or can I use my US one? (2) What documentation do I need to save for insurance reimbursement? (3) How do I get an itemized receipt in English? (4) What's the typical reimbursement process for foreign eyewear purchases?

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps, pin top 3 Kyoto temples
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are worth waking up early for?
tasks.reward: Turns remaining Kyoto time into a curated experience, not aimless wandering
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, best before 8am
Arashiyama bamboo grove magical at sunrise
tasks.system_prompt: I'm in Kyoto right now (day 12 of 16-day Japan trip, leaving April 7). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, food. Already stayed here a few days but want to fill remaining time with top experiences. Give me a curated list of 4-5 must-do Kyoto activities with: opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, best time to visit, and why each is worth it. Mix major landmarks with hidden gems.

tasks.hook_line: Takayama is mountain town — are there hidden hiking trails or just tourist shrines?
tasks.reward: Ensures Takayama isn't just a transit stop between Kyoto and Tokyo
nuggets: Old town Sanmachi district, Edo-period streets
tasks.system_prompt: I'm traveling through Takayama, Japan (mountain town between Kyoto and Tokyo) in the next few days. Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like hiking, outdoor spaces, local food, and avoiding pure tourist traps. Give me 3-4 Takayama activities worth doing: include opening hours, costs, Google Maps links, and whether they're walkable from town center. Looking for mix of nature and culture.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps, search Tokyo neighborhoods
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — which neighborhoods match your vibe vs pure chaos?
tasks.reward: Creates a Tokyo plan that feels intentional, not overwhelming
nuggets: Shimokitazawa for vintage shops, indie vibes
Tsukiji Outer Market for breakfast sushi 6am
tasks.system_prompt: I'm ending my Japan trip in Tokyo (arriving soon, leaving April 7). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like food, walking interesting neighborhoods, quirky experiences, and we're already doing fountain pen shopping. Tokyo is huge — help me pick 5-6 specific things to do that aren't just 'visit Shibuya.' Give me neighborhood names, specific spots, opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested order based on geography. We like local flavor over tourist spectacle.

tasks.first_domino: Open terminal, git clone Cerebro repo
tasks.hook_line: Can you get the dev portal running before anyone notices you're unblocking yourself?
tasks.reward: Unparks the entire Cerebro project — goes from warm to hot
nuggets: May need Docker or local DB setup
tasks.system_prompt: I need to get Cerebro (a developer portal for internal engineers) running locally on my machine. I'm a frontend engineer. Walk me through the setup process step-by-step: clone repo, install dependencies, configure environment variables, start dev server. Assume I have Node, Docker, and standard dev tools installed. If there's a README, summarize the key steps. If setup fails, help me debug common issues.

recommended_slots.reason: 3 days left in Japan, glasses shopping is time-sensitive and blocks other activities
Kyoto planning creates immediate value for today/tomorrow, medium effort fits ramp_up energy
Quick research win that clarifies whether glasses shopping is worth the effort
